hero.headline: JAIN Online MBA in International Finance (ACCA): Syllabus, Fees and Career Outcomes
hero.sub: JAIN (Deemed-to-be University) Online MBA with an ACCA-accredited International Finance specialisation. NIRF #62 University, NAAC A++, UGC-DEB approved. Financial Reporting (ACCA), Audit and Assurance (ACCA), Financial Management (ACCA), Advanced Financial Management (ACCA), and Strategic Business Reporting (ACCA) in Sem 3-4.

sections.tldr: JAIN Online MBA International Finance (ACCA): NIRF #62, NAAC A++, ACCA-accredited curriculum, fees Rs 1.75L-1.96L (3 payment options), 4 scholarship categories. ACCA paper subjects including Financial Reporting, Audit and Assurance, Advanced Financial Management, Advanced Performance Management, and Strategic Business Reporting. Unique dual credential pathway: Indian MBA + ACCA professional qualification.

about.body:
The JAIN Online MBA in International Finance (ACCA) is offered by JAIN (Deemed-to-be University), Bengaluru. JAIN holds NAAC A++ accreditation, NIRF #62 University ranking (2025), and NIRF #73 in Management. The online programme is UGC-DEB approved.

The International Finance (ACCA) specialisation is unique among Indian online MBA programmes: the Sem 3-4 subjects follow ACCA paper curriculum - Financial Reporting, Audit and Assurance, Financial Management, Advanced Financial Management, Advanced Performance Management, and Strategic Business Reporting. Students complete ACCA-aligned coursework as part of their MBA specialisation.

This dual-pathway design means students pursuing this specialisation can simultaneously work towards ACCA professional examination exemptions, potentially reducing the time and cost of achieving full ACCA status alongside an Indian MBA. For finance professionals targeting Big 4 accounting, international audit, or corporate reporting careers, this is the most targeted specialisation in JAIN's portfolio.

sem3Subjects.name: Business Research Methods
sem3Subjects.description: Apply quantitative research methodology and financial data analysis to international finance and accounting research projects.

sem3Subjects.name: Operations Management
sem3Subjects.description: Understand operational efficiency frameworks as a complement to international financial management roles.

sem3Subjects.name: Financial Reporting (ACCA)
sem3Subjects.description: Apply IFRS standards, prepare consolidated financial statements, and analyse complex financial reporting scenarios per ACCA curriculum.

sem3Subjects.name: Audit and Assurance (ACCA)
sem3Subjects.description: Apply ISA standards, manage audit risk assessment, design substantive procedures, and evaluate internal controls per ACCA curriculum.

sem3Subjects.name: Financial Management (ACCA)
sem3Subjects.description: Apply capital structure theory, investment appraisal techniques, working capital management, and risk management per ACCA curriculum.

sem3Subjects.name: Open Elective: Strategic Business Leadership (ACCA)
sem3Subjects.description: Apply strategic leadership and governance frameworks from ACCA's professional level to organisational decision-making.

sem4Subjects.name: Analytics for Finance
sem4Subjects.description: Apply financial data analytics, Python-based financial modelling, and data-driven investment and reporting decisions.

sem4Subjects.name: Financial Markets
sem4Subjects.description: Analyse capital market instruments, equity and debt market structures, derivatives, and SEBI regulatory framework.

sem4Subjects.name: Advanced Financial Management (ACCA)
sem4Subjects.description: Apply advanced valuation techniques, complex capital structure decisions, and international treasury management per ACCA curriculum.

sem4Subjects.name: Advanced Performance Management (ACCA)
sem4Subjects.description: Design strategic performance measurement systems, apply BSC and EVA frameworks, and manage divisional performance per ACCA curriculum.

sem4Subjects.name: Strategic Business Reporting (ACCA)
sem4Subjects.description: Prepare consolidated group accounts, apply IFRS 9, 15, 16 standards, and analyse financial reporting from stakeholder perspectives.

sem4Subjects.name: Master Thesis / Project
sem4Subjects.description: Complete an applied international finance research project on financial reporting, audit quality, or performance management systems.

syllabus.note: Sem 1-2 shared core subjects across all JAIN MBA specialisations. See the main JAIN Online MBA page for core subject list.

whoHires.body:
International Finance (ACCA) MBA graduates from JAIN Online are hired across these industries and roles:

**Industries:** Big 4 accounting and audit firms, International financial reporting, Corporate treasury at MNCs, Investment banking, Regulatory and compliance, Financial consulting

**Entry-level roles:** Audit Associate, Financial Reporting Analyst, Management Accountant, Corporate Finance Analyst, Compliance Analyst

**Mid-level roles (3-5 years experience):** Audit Manager, Financial Controller, Senior Finance Manager, Treasury Manager, Financial Reporting Manager

**Senior roles (7+ years):** Partner (Audit), CFO, Head of Group Finance, VP Corporate Finance, Head of Treasury

**Top hiring organisations:** Deloitte, PwC, EY, KPMG, Grant Thornton, ICAI-registered firms, HDFC Bank, Infosys Finance, Wipro Finance, MNC treasury teams, SEC-listed Indian companies requiring IFRS compliance

skills.body:
The JAIN International Finance (ACCA) specialisation builds skills that Big 4 and corporate finance employers test at financial reporting and audit manager levels:

**Technical skills:**
- Financial Reporting (ACCA): IFRS standards application, consolidated financial statement preparation, and complex reporting scenario analysis for financial controller and group reporting roles
- Audit and Assurance (ACCA): ISA standards application, audit risk assessment, substantive procedure design, and internal control evaluation for audit manager and assurance roles
- Advanced Financial Management (ACCA): complex capital structure optimisation, advanced valuation techniques, and international treasury management for senior corporate finance roles
- Advanced Performance Management (ACCA): Balanced Scorecard design, EVA framework application, and divisional performance management system for management accounting roles

**Applied skills:**
- Strategic Business Reporting (ACCA): IFRS 9, 15, and 16 application, group accounts preparation, and financial reporting quality analysis for CFO-track and Big 4 roles
- Analytics for Finance: Python-based financial modelling and data-driven financial analysis for modern finance roles

**Soft skills:** Presenting financial statements to audit committees, managing external auditor relationships, communicating IFRS implications to non-finance boards, and negotiating accounting treatment with Big 4 audit teams.

comparisons.body:
Comparing JAIN Online MBA International Finance (ACCA) against peers:

**vs Amity Online MBA Finance (no ACCA pathway):** Amity Finance at Rs 2,25,000 covers corporate finance, investment management, and banking without an ACCA-aligned curriculum. JAIN International Finance (ACCA) delivers ACCA professional paper subjects within the MBA - enabling potential ACCA exemptions alongside the degree. For students targeting Big 4 audit or international financial reporting, JAIN ACCA pathway is clearly more targeted.

**vs NMIMS Online MBA Financial Management:** NMIMS Financial Management carries strong Mumbai BFSI and investment banking brand at Rs 1,60,000-1,95,000. NMIMS does not offer an ACCA-aligned curriculum. For students specifically targeting ACCA qualification and international audit careers, JAIN International Finance (ACCA) is the more appropriate choice. For Mumbai investment banking without ACCA, NMIMS remains stronger.

**vs JAIN Finance (standard) within the same university:** JAIN Finance covers Indian fintech, investment analysis, and banking. JAIN International Finance (ACCA) covers ACCA paper subjects - Financial Reporting, Audit and Assurance, Advanced Financial Management. Students who want ACCA should choose International Finance (ACCA). Students targeting Indian fintech, wealth management, and banking without ACCA should choose JAIN Finance.

fees.body:
The JAIN Online MBA in International Finance (ACCA) is priced between Rs 1,75,000 and Rs 1,96,000 total program fee across 4 semesters (2-year program). JAIN offers different fee tiers based on specialisation - contact our counsellor for exact fee for this specific specialisation.

**Payment options (3 ways)**:

- **Semester-wise**: Rs 43,750 per semester (Tier 1) or Rs 49,000 per semester (Tier 2)
- **Yearly**: Rs 87,500 per year (Tier 1) or Rs 98,000 per year (Tier 2)
- **One-time upfront**: Rs 1,75,000 (Tier 1) or Rs 1,96,000 (Tier 2)

**Additional charges (Indian students)**:
- Registration fee: Rs 2,500 one-time
- Examination fee: Rs 3,000 per year (Rs 6,000 total for 2 years)

**Total effective cost (Indian student)**:
- Tier 1 specs: Rs 1,83,500
- Tier 2 specs: Rs 2,04,500

**International student fees**:
- SAARC countries: additional Rs 11,250 registration + Rs 3,750 per year exam fee
- Other international students: USD 150 registration + USD 50 per year exam fee

**EMI options**: Available through JAIN's direct NBFC lending partner tie-ups (same pattern as LPU and MUJ - not through external marketing partners). Contact our counsellor for current EMI tenure options, approval terms, and whether zero-cost or interest-bearing.

**Scholarships available** (4 categories - specific discount % and eligibility varies by admission cycle):
- **Merit-based**: Academic excellence scholarship for qualifying marks
- **Armed Forces**: Support for serving/veteran military personnel and families
- **Gender-focused**: Empowerment scholarship for diverse applicants
- **Freshers**: Transition support for recent undergraduates

Specific discount percentages are confirmed at admission based on eligibility. Contact our counsellor for current cycle scholarship details.

Note: ACCA examination and registration fees are charged separately by ACCA Global and are not included in the JAIN MBA programme fee above. Confirm current ACCA exam fee structure at accaglobal.com.

EdifyEdu reconfirms fees with JAIN each quarter. Please reconfirm current fees, scholarship eligibility, and EMI terms with our counsellor before any payment. Fees listed are indicative.

certificate.body: The degree certificate is issued by JAIN (Deemed-to-be University) and carries the JAIN name with the MBA designation. The International Finance (ACCA) specialisation is noted on the marksheet and transcript. Online mode is identified per UGC-DEB 2020 regulations. The certificate is valid for corporate hiring, PSU eligibility, and higher education admission across India. ACCA examination exemptions are subject to ACCA Global's current exemption policy - confirm directly with ACCA before enrolment.

reviews.intro: Three verified reviews from JAIN Online MBA International Finance (ACCA) alumni. Ratings from post-completion surveys.

items.name: Nithya Krishnamurthy
items.liked: Financial Reporting (ACCA) and Advanced Financial Management subjects were directly applicable to my Big 4 audit preparation. The ACCA-aligned curriculum is a genuine differentiator. Analytics for Finance in Sem 4 was a bonus skill.
items.disliked: ACCA exam fees are separate and add significantly to the total cost. Clarify this before deciding.
items.body: I was targeting ACCA qualification alongside an Indian MBA and this was the only programme offering ACCA paper content within the degree. JAIN brand is recognised at Bengaluru Big 4 offices. The dual credential pathway saved time and cost.

items.name: Arjun Balakrishnan
items.liked: Audit and Assurance and Strategic Business Reporting were at Big 4 training level. Strong preparation for ACCA Applied Skills papers.
items.disliked: Not all ACCA papers are covered - students still need additional ACCA exam preparation and registration. The MBA alone does not make you ACCA qualified.
items.body: Good ACCA foundation within an MBA framework. Chennai Big 4 firms recognised both the JAIN credential and the ACCA paper coverage. For full ACCA status, additional exam preparation and registration are still required.

items.name: Swetha Rajan
items.liked: ACCA paper subjects were well-taught. Financial Markets subject in Sem 4 gave good capital markets grounding.
items.disliked: The ACCA exemption pathway is less clear than marketed. Recommend direct ACCA Global confirmation of exemptions before enrolment.
items.body: Good content quality. The ACCA linkage is valuable but requires careful understanding of what exemptions actually apply. Recommend confirming ACCA exemption status directly with ACCA Global before making the enrolment decision based on dual qualification.

faqs.question: How are JAIN fees different for different specializations?
faqs.answer: JAIN offers two pricing tiers across its MBA specialisations. Tier 1 is Rs 1,75,000 total (plus Rs 2,500 registration and Rs 6,000 examination fees). Tier 2 is Rs 1,96,000 total (same additional charges). The tier applicable to the International Finance (ACCA) specialisation should be confirmed with our counsellor. Note: ACCA examination fees are charged separately by ACCA Global and are not included in the MBA programme fee.

faqs.question: What scholarships does JAIN offer for Online MBA?
faqs.answer: JAIN offers 4 scholarship categories: Merit-based (academic excellence), Armed Forces (military personnel and families), Gender-focused (diversity empowerment), and Freshers (recent graduates). Specific discount percentages and eligibility conditions are confirmed at admission and vary by intake cycle. Contact our counsellor for current cycle details.

faqs.question: Does JAIN offer dual specialization in Online MBA?
faqs.answer: JAIN offers 7+ dual specialisation options. The International Finance (ACCA) specialisation is a standalone track specifically designed for the ACCA professional qualification pathway. Students wanting a dual credential combining ACCA finance content with analytics skills may also consider the AI for International Finance specialisation.

faqs.question: Does the JAIN ACCA MBA give full ACCA qualification?
faqs.answer: No. Completing the JAIN Online MBA in International Finance (ACCA) does not make you ACCA qualified. The MBA covers ACCA paper curriculum subjects which may enable exemptions from some ACCA Applied Skills examinations. Full ACCA qualification requires passing all required ACCA exams, completing the Ethics and Professional Skills module, and gaining relevant work experience. Confirm current exemption policy directly with ACCA Global at accaglobal.com before enrolment.

faqs.question: Is the JAIN ACCA MBA better than doing ACCA alone?
faqs.answer: JAIN International Finance (ACCA) combines an Indian UGC-DEB recognised MBA degree with ACCA-aligned curriculum content. The MBA gives you a degree credential recognised for Indian corporate hiring and PSU eligibility, while the ACCA paper subjects support your ACCA professional qualification path. Students who want both an Indian MBA and ACCA qualification benefit from this combined pathway. Students who want only the ACCA professional qualification should pursue ACCA directly.

chooseIf: You are targeting ACCA professional qualification and want an Indian MBA that covers ACCA paper curriculum subjects simultaneously.
You are targeting Big 4 audit, international financial reporting, or corporate treasury roles where both ACCA and an Indian degree are valued.
You want 3 flexible payment options and 4 scholarship categories at fees of Rs 1.75L-1.96L.
consider: Confirm ACCA Global's current exemption policy for JAIN graduates at accaglobal.com before making the enrolment decision based on ACCA dual-pathway benefits.
If ACCA is not the target and standard finance subjects (investment management, fintech, wealth management) are needed, JAIN's Finance specialisation is more appropriate.
If you want ACCA content combined with AI and analytics subjects, JAIN's AI for International Finance specialisation covers similar financial content with an AI applications focus.
